## plugin:context7:context7
Use this server to fetch current documentation whenever the user asks about a library, framework, SDK, API, CLI tool, or cloud service -- even well-known ones like React, Next.js, Prisma, Express, Tailwind, Django, or Spring Boot. This includes API syntax, configuration, version migration, library-specific debugging, setup instructions, and CLI tool usage. Use even when you think you know the answer -- your training data may not reflect recent changes. Prefer this over web search for library docs.

Do not use for: refactoring, writing scripts from scratch, debugging business logic, code review, or general programming concepts.

## plugin:figma:figma
The official Figma MCP server. Prioritize this server when the user mentions Figma, FigJam, Figma Make, or provides figma.com URLs.

Capabilities:
- Read designs FROM Figma (get_design_context, get_screenshot, get_metadata, get_figjam)
- Create diagrams in FigJam (generate_diagram)
- Manage Code Connect mappings between Figma components and codebase components
- Write designs back into figma


WHEN TO USE THESE TOOLS:
- The user shares a Figma URL (figma.com/design/..., figma.com/board/..., figma.com/make/...)
- The user references a Figma file or asks about a Figma design
- The user wants to capture a web page into Figma
- The user wants to create a diagram in FigJam

URL PARSING:
Extract fileKey and nodeId from Figma URLs:
- figma.com/design/:fileKey/:fileName?node-id=:nodeId → convert "-" to ":" in nodeId
- figma.com/design/:fileKey/branch/:branchKey/:fileName → use branchKey as fileKey
- figma.com/make/:makeFileKey/:makeFileName → use makeFileKey
- figma.com/board/:fileKey/:fileName → FigJam file, use get_figjam

DESIGN-TO-CODE WORKFLOW:

Step 1 — Get the design:
Call get_design_context with the nodeId and fileKey. This is your primary tool.
It returns code, a screenshot, and contextual hints.

Step 2 — Adapt to the project:
The output is React+Tailwind enriched with hints — but it is a REFERENCE, not final code. Always adapt to the target project's stack, components, and conventions.
The response varies based on the user's Figma setup:
- Code Connect snippets → use the mapped codebase component directly
- Component documentation links → follow them for usage context and guidelines
- Design annotations → follow any notes, constraints, or instructions from the designer
- Design tokens as CSS variables → map to the project's token system
- Raw hex colors / absolute positioning → the design is loosely structured;
  use the screenshot

Check the target project for existing components, layout patterns,and tokens that match the design intent. Reuse what the project already has instead of generating new code from sc… [truncated]

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- misc:code-explain: Code Explanation and Analysis
- misc:lint: Run linting and fix code quality issues
- misc:docs-generate: Generate documentation for code, APIs, and components
- misc:code-optimize: Analyze and optimize code for performance, memory, and efficiency
- misc:code-cleanup: Refactor and clean up code following best practices
- misc:feature-plan: Plan feature implementation with technical specifications
- ui:page-new: Create a new Next.js page with App Router best practices
- ui:component-new: Create a new React component with TypeScript and modern best practices
- supabase:types-gen: Generate TypeScript types from Supabase database schema
- supabase:edge-function-new: Create a new Supabase Edge Function with Deno
- new-task: Analyze task complexity and create actionable implementation plan
- api:api-test: Test API endpoints with automated test generation
- api:api-protect: Add authentication, authorization, and security to API endpoints
- api:api-new: Create a new Next.js API route with validation, error handling, and TypeScript
- code-review:code-review: Code review a pull request
- feature-dev:feature-dev: Guided feature development with codebase understanding and architecture focus
- frontend-design:frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, or applicat…
- figma:figma-generate-design: Use this skill alongside figma-use when the task involves translating an application page, view, or multi-section layout into Figma. Triggers: 'write to Figma',…
- figma:figma-create-design-system-rules: Generates custom design system rules for the user's codebase. Use when user says "create design system rules", "generate rules for my project", "set up design r…
- figma:figma-code-connect: Creates and maintains Figma Code Connect template files that map Figma components to code snippets. Use when the user mentions Code Connect, Figma component map…
- figma:figma-use: **MANDATORY prerequisite** — you MUST invoke this skill BEFORE every `use_figma` tool call. NEVER call `use_figma` directly without loading this skill first. Sk…
- figma:figma-implement-design: Translates Figma designs into production-ready application code with 1:1 visual fidelity. Use when implementing UI code from Figma files, when user mentions "im…
- figma:figma-use-figjam: This skill helps agents use Figma's use_figma MCP tool in the FigJam context. Can be used alongside figma-use which has foundational context for using the use_f…
- figma:figma-generate-diagram: MANDATORY prerequisite — load this skill BEFORE every `generate_diagram` tool call. Routes to type-specific guidance (generic flowchart, architecture flowchart)…
- figma:figma-generate-library: Build or update a professional-grade design system in Figma from a codebase. Use when the user wants to create variables/tokens, build component libraries, set …
- claude-code-setup:claude-automation-recommender: Analyze a codebase and recommend Claude Code automations (hooks, subagents, skills, plugins, MCP servers). Use when user asks for automation recommendations, wa…
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: Let me explore the project structure to understand where to implement these meta tags.

input.description: Explore meta tags implementation in Next.js app
input.prompt:
Explore the Next.js App Router project at /Users/ilyakonyaev/work/coddtech/lux/luxflow-fe to understand:
1. Where the root layout is (app/layout.tsx or similar)
2. How metadata is currently configured (any existing `metadata` exports or `<Head>` usage)
3. Where the OG image would be stored (public folder structure)
4. The app directory structure

Report file paths and relevant code snippets. Be thorough but concise.

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create my final report:

## LuxFlow Frontend Project Structure Analysis

### 1. **Root Layout Location**
**File:** `/Users/ilyakonyaev/work/coddtech/lux/luxflow-fe/app/layout.tsx`

- Uses App Router with Next.js 15.5.9
- Exports `Metadata` object (line 38-60)
- Contains font configuration (Inter, Roboto, Inria Serif, DM Sans)
- Wraps children with providers (AllProviders, ToastProvider)

### 2. **Metadata Configuration**

**Current metadata in root layout:**
```typescript
export const metadata: Metadata = {
  title: "Luxury Asset Liquidity",
  description: "Luxury Asset Liquidity",
  manifest: "/site.webmanifest",
  icons: [
    {
      sizes: "32x32",
      href: "/favicon-32x32.png",
      url: "/favicon-32x32.png",
    },
    {
      sizes: "16x16",
      href: "/favicon-16x16.png",
      url: "/favicon-16x16.png",
    },
    {
      rel: "apple-touch-icon",
      href: "/apple-touch-icon.png",
      url: "/favicon-16x16.png",
      sizes: "180x180",
    },
  ],
};
```

**Additional metadata usage:**
- Individual admin pages export metadata (e.g., `/app/(app)/admin/page.tsx` has title: "Admin Dashboard")
- No OG image or Twitter card metadata currently configured anywhere
- No `generateMetadata` functions found (static metadata only)

### 3. **Public Folder Structure for OG Images**

**Public directory path:** `/Users/ilyakonyaev/work/coddtech/lux/luxflow-fe/public/`

**Key subdirectories:**
- `/public/new-design-images/` - 48 subdirectories with brand images, carousel, auth assets
- `/public/fonts/` - Custom fonts
- `/public/mock-images/` - Mock data images
- `/public/new-design-videos/` - Video assets
- `/public/widgets/` - Widget assets
- `/public/lottie/` - Animation files

**Existing image files:**
- Favicons: `favicon-32x32.png`, `favicon-16x16.png`, `favicon.ico`
- Apple touch icon: `apple-touch-icon.png` (180x180)
- Android Chrome icons: `android-chrome-192x192.png`, `android-chrome-512x512.png`
- Manifest: `site.webmanifest`

**Recommended OG image location:** `/public/og-image.png` (standard Next.js convention)

### 4. **App Directory Structure**

```
/app/
├── layout.tsx (root layout with metadata)
├── api/
│   └── auth/[...nextauth]/
├── (marketing) - route group
│   ├── layout.tsx (wraps with Header, Footer, MarketingMain)
│   ├── page.tsx (home page)
│   ├── about/
│   ├── contact/
│   ├── faq/
│   ├── pricing/
│   ├── services/
│   │   ├── buyback/
│   │   └── permanent-sale/
│   └── [other pages]
├── (auth) - route group
│   ├── layout.tsx (uses AuthLayout widget)
│   ├── sign-in/
│   ├── register/
│   └── [other auth routes]
└── (app) - route group (protected routes)
    ├── layout.tsx (uses AuthenticatedLayout widget)
    ├── (protected) - nested route group
    │   ├── layout.tsx (sidebar layout with NEW_DESIGN_ROUTES)
    │   ├── buybacks/
    │   ├── my-offers/
    │   ├── profile/
    │   ├── wallet/
    │   └── [other protected routes]
    ├── admin/ - route group
    │   ├── layout.tsx (admin sidebar layout)
    │   ├── page.tsx (with metadata)
    │   ├── users/
    │   ├── payment-management/
    │   ├── finance/
    │   └── [other admin routes]
    ├── authenticator/
    └── support/
```

### 5. **Key Observations**

- **Route groups used:** `(marketing)`, `(auth)`, `(app)`, `(protected)`, `admin` for different layout contexts
- **Metadata pattern:** Only root layout has full metadata; child pages export simple Metadata objects
- **No OG implementation:** Project has no openGraph, twitter, or og:image configuration
- **Next.js Config:** `/next.config.ts` has unoptimized images, remote patterns allowed, AVIF/WebP formats enabled
- **Marketing layout:** Uses custom `<MarketingMain>` component with Header and Footer widgets

- **Route groups used:** `(marketing)`, `(auth)`, `(app)`, `(protected)`, `admin` for different layout contexts
- **Metadata pattern:** Only root layout has full metadata; child pages export simple Metadata objects
- **No OG implementation:** Project has no openGraph, twitter, or og:image configuration
- **Next.js Config:** `/next.config.ts` has unoptimized images, remote patterns allowed, AVIF/WebP formats enabled
- **Marketing layout:** Uses custom `<MarketingMain>` component with Header and Footer widgets

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user wants to implement SEO metadata for the LuxFlow frontend application. They provided a table specifying metadata for the "Common" (global/default) configuration:
   - **Meta Title**: "LuxFlow | Fast Liquidity for Luxury Watches & Valuables"
   - **Meta Description**: "Unlock fast liquidity from luxury watches, handbags, and high-value items with secure authentication, AI, expert-grade valuation, and flexible buyback options."
   - **OG Title**: "Unlock Instant Liquidity from Your Luxury Items | LuxFlow"
   - **OG Description**: "Get fast, secure and discreet liquidity from luxury watches, handbags, and high-value items with flexible buyback options."
   - **OG Image**: A PNG image (shown as base64 and as Image #3 — appears to be a Rolex watch photo with "Unlock instant liquidity from your luxury items" text overlay)
   
   The table header row has "Common" with no URL, suggesting this is the global/root-level metadata applied to all pages by default.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Next.js 15.5.9 App Router metadata API (`export const metadata: Metadata`)
   - OpenGraph (OG) meta tags for social sharing
   - Static metadata configuration in root layout
   - Next.js public folder for static assets (OG images)
   - Route groups in Next.js App Router: `(marketing)`, `(auth)`, `(app)`, `(protected)`
   - Feature-Sliced Design (FSD) architecture used in the project

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - **`/Users/ilyakonyaev/work/coddtech/lux/luxflow-fe/app/layout.tsx`**
     - Root layout file containing the current metadata export
     - This is where the global/common metadata needs to be updated
     - Current metadata (needs to be replaced/updated):
       ```typescript
       export const metadata: Metadata = {
         title: "Luxury Asset Liquidity",
         description: "Luxury Asset Liquidity",
         manifest: "/site.webmanifest",
         icons: [
           {
             sizes: "32x32",
             href: "/favicon-32x32.png",
             url: "/favicon-32x32.png",
           },
           {
             sizes: "16x16",
             href: "/favicon-16x16.png",
             url: "/favicon-16x16.png",
           },
           {
             rel: "apple-touch-icon",
             href: "/apple-touch-icon.png",
             url: "/favicon-16x16.png",
             sizes: "180x180",
           },
         ],
       };
       ```

   - **`/Users/ilyakonyaev/work/coddtech/lux/luxflow-fe/public/`**
     - Public folder where the OG image needs to be saved
     - Recommended path: `/public/og-image.png`
     - Currently contains: favicons, android chrome icons, site.webmanifest, and various subdirectories (`new-design-images/`, `fonts/`, `mock-images/`, `new-design-videos/`, `widgets/`, `lottie/`)

4. Errors and fixes:
   - No errors encountered yet; exploration phase was successful.

5. Problem Solving:
   - Successfully explored the project structure to identify that:
     - Root metadata lives in `app/layout.tsx`
     - No OG metadata is currently configured anywhere in the project
     - Individual pages use simple `export const metadata` objects (e.g., admin page has `title: "Admin Dashboard"`)
     - No `generateMetadata` functions are used — all metadata is static
   - The OG image (provided as base64) needs to be extracted and saved as a file in `/public/`

6. All user messages:
   - The user's single message contained a metadata specification table requesting implementation of SEO meta tags for the LuxFlow app, with columns: Page, URL, Meta Title, Meta Description, OG Title, OG Description, OG Image. The "Common" row (global defaults) was provided with all values filled in, including a large base64-encoded PNG as the OG image.

7. Pending Tasks:
   - Save the OG image to `/public/og-image.png` (decode the base64 PNG from the user's message)
   - Update `app/layout.tsx` to include the new metadata:
     - Change `title` to "LuxFlow | Fast Liquidity for Luxury Watches & Valuables"
     - Change `description` to the full meta description provided
     - Add `openGraph` object with title, description, images pointing to `/og-image.png`
     - Optionally add `twitter` card metadata

8. Current Work:
   Immediately before this summary, I had just completed the codebase exploration via an Explore agent. The agent confirmed:
   - The target file for changes is `app/layout.tsx`
   - The current metadata is minimal (only title "Luxury Asset Liquidity", description "Luxury Asset Liquidity", manifest, and icons)
   - No OG/openGraph metadata exists anywhere in the project
   - The OG image should be placed at `/public/og-image.png`
   
   No file edits have been made yet — the task is ready to implement.

9. Optional Next Step:
   The next step is to implement the metadata changes. Based on the user's request ("надо сделать вот такое" = "need to do this"):
   
   1. Save the OG image from the base64 data to `/public/og-image.png`
   2. Update `app/layout.tsx` metadata export to:
   ```typescript
   export const metadata: Metadata = {
     title: "LuxFlow | Fast Liquidity for Luxury Watches & Valuables",
     description: "Unlock fast liquidity from luxury watches, handbags, and high-value items with secure authentication, AI, expert-grade valuation, and flexible buyback options.",
     manifest: "/site.webmanifest",
     openGraph: {
       title: "Unlock Instant Liquidity from Your Luxury Items | LuxFlow",
       description: "Get fast, secure and discreet liquidity from luxury watches, handbags, and high-value items with flexible buyback options.",
       images: [{ url: "/og-image.png" }],
     },
     icons: [...], // keep existing icons
   };
   ```

If you need specific details from before compaction (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: /Users/ilyakonyaev/.claude/projects/-Users-ilyakonyaev-work-coddtech-lux-luxflow-fe/00b407ff-d94b-47ac-9277-1d4de4a77901.jsonl
Continue the conversation from where it left off without asking the user any further questions. Resume directly — do not acknowledge the summary, do not recap what was happening, do not preface with "I'll continue" or similar. Pick up the last task as if the break never happened.

addedBlocks:
## plugin:context7:context7
Use this server to fetch current documentation whenever the user asks about a library, framework, SDK, API, CLI tool, or cloud service -- even well-known ones like React, Next.js, Prisma, Express, Tailwind, Django, or Spring Boot. This includes API syntax, configuration, version migration, library-specific debugging, setup instructions, and CLI tool usage. Use even when you think you know the answer -- your training data may not reflect recent changes. Prefer this over web search for library docs.

Do not use for: refactoring, writing scripts from scratch, debugging business logic, code review, or general programming concepts.

## plugin:figma:figma
The official Figma MCP server. Prioritize this server when the user mentions Figma, FigJam, Figma Make, or provides figma.com URLs.

Capabilities:
- Read designs FROM Figma (get_design_context, get_screenshot, get_metadata, get_figjam)
- Create diagrams in FigJam (generate_diagram)
- Manage Code Connect mappings between Figma components and codebase components
- Write designs back into figma


WHEN TO USE THESE TOOLS:
- The user shares a Figma URL (figma.com/design/..., figma.com/board/..., figma.com/make/...)
- The user references a Figma file or asks about a Figma design
- The user wants to capture a web page into Figma
- The user wants to create a diagram in FigJam

URL PARSING:
Extract fileKey and nodeId from Figma URLs:
- figma.com/design/:fileKey/:fileName?node-id=:nodeId → convert "-" to ":" in nodeId
- figma.com/design/:fileKey/branch/:branchKey/:fileName → use branchKey as fileKey
- figma.com/make/:makeFileKey/:makeFileName → use makeFileKey
- figma.com/board/:fileKey/:fileName → FigJam file, use get_figjam

DESIGN-TO-CODE WORKFLOW:

Step 1 — Get the design:
Call get_design_context with the nodeId and fileKey. This is your primary tool.
It returns code, a screenshot, and contextual hints.

Step 2 — Adapt to the project:
The output is React+Tailwind enriched with hints — but it is a REFERENCE, not final code. Always adapt to the target project's stack, components, and conventions.
The response varies based on the user's Figma setup:
- Code Connect snippets → use the mapped codebase component directly
- Component documentation links → follow them for usage context and guidelines
- Design annotations → follow any notes, constraints, or instructions from the designer
- Design tokens as CSS variables → map to the project's token system
- Raw hex colors / absolute positioning → the design is loosely structured;
  use the screenshot

Check the target project for existing components, layout patterns,and tokens that match the design intent. Reuse what the project already has instead of generating new code from sc… [truncated]
